title: Local desktop agent client
scenario: Build a Claude Desktop-style local agent client with MCP trust boundaries, file grants, skills, conversations and AG-UI events, model routing, approval denial, audit replay, and public workflow proof.
termination: Stop only when a permissioned MCP workflow succeeds, a denied approval is enforced, file grants are scoped, conversations/events are persisted and replayable, model routing is explained from the registry, and the UI proves the public workflow.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify desktop shell, local persistence, MCP host strategy, file-grant model, skill directory, model registry, and at least one safe MCP server for proof.
prompts.discovery: Read agent protocol docs, MCP trust model, local skill loading rules, model registry, Tauri/Rust patterns, UI standard, and conversation/event store docs.
prompts.feynman: Explain how MCP server trust, file grants, model routing, skills, approvals, conversations, and audit replay fit together without granting broad filesystem authority.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess trusted/untrusted tools, file grants, approval UX, denial behavior, model routing, conversation history, event replay, and offline/local behavior.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze current MCP host, permission store, skill loader, conversation/event schema, model registry, UI timeline, and Tauri command boundaries.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify MCP server registry, per-tool permissions, file grants, approval/denial events, conversation store, model route selection, audit replay, and UI proof.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan permission/domain types first, then MCP host, skill loader, conversation/event persistence, chat UI, denial flow, replay, verification, critic, and retention.
prompts.research: Use official MCP/ACP/AG-UI/local protocol docs and local source to confirm current protocol contracts and security expectations.
prompts.implementation: Implement one safe public workflow. Require explicit file grants, persist approvals and denials, route models from the dated registry, and render the full event chain.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Run a tool through the UI with a granted permission, deny another request, show enforcement, persist the conversation/events, and replay the audit trail after restart.
prompts.independent_critic: Review trust boundaries, file grants, denial enforcement, skill loading, model routing, event completeness, replay, and whether the workflow is real rather than mocked.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record protocol decisions, permission failures, model-routing gaps, replay evidence, and reusable agent-client lessons.
prompts.recovery: If denial enforcement or replay fails twice, stop and repair the permission/event model before adding more tool integrations.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for broad file grants, untrusted MCP execution, missing denial proof, non-replayable conversations, stale model claims, or simulated workflow evidence.
